meaning: n.[基督教]门诺(Mennon)派中的严紧派adj.[基督教]门诺(Mennon)派中的严紧派
examples: These Amish cushions in traditional deep colours are available in two sizes.
Most people have no quarrel with the Amish for doing things the old-fashioned way.
The Old Order Amish, who arrived on American shores in colonial times, has survived in the modern world in distinctive, small communities.
The researchers do not include groups that drive automobiles, such as Beachy Amish and Amish Mennonites.
East Lampeter Township, in Pennsylvania Amish country, has about 13, 500 residents, according to the 2000 census.
